title: Chrysanthemum nitens
intro: Chrysanthemum nitens, also known as the shining chrysanthemum, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to East Asia. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum nitens include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and improve immune function. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, as well as sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Chrysanthemum nitens is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum nitens is classified as a perennial herbaceous plant within the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a member of the genus Chrysanthemum, which is a group of flowering plants native to East Asia. The species Chrysanthemum nitens is further divided into several subspecies based on its geographical distribution and morphological characteristics. Chrysanthemum nitens is often grouped together with other Chrysanthemum species in the subgenus Chrysanthemum. It is closely related to other species within the Asteraceae family, including Calendula and Bellis.
uses: Chrysanthemum nitens is traditionally used in Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its purported health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever and digestive issues. The plant is also thought to possess antioxidant properties, which could contribute to its potential anti-aging effects. Furthermore, it is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. However, the efficacy and safety of these uses have not been extensively scientifically studied.
